title: The Henry Art Gallery’s free admission model increases visitors by 55% - The Daily
content: The exterior of the Henry Art Gallery is pictured Oct. 23, 2025, in Seattle. # The Henry Art Gallery’s free admission model increases visitors by 55%. By  Macey Wurm The Daily. In May 2025, the Henry Art Gallery introduced a free admission model for all prospective visitors. Free admission was adopted to further the gallery’s goals of generating an inclusive and diverse audience base, as well as to increase access for everyone to have the opportunity to build a relationship with the gallery and the presented works. The grant awarded to the gallery covers previous revenue generated by admission costs for the next five years, and the gallery has an opportunity to reapply for the grant at the conclusion of this term. Founded in 1926 as Washington state’s first art museum, the decision for the Henry to adopt free admission as it nears its centennial marks a new era for the gallery.

title: Hours & Admission - Henry Art Gallery
content: The Henry is a museum for contemporary art and ideas, grounded in the belief that art has the power to challenge norms, inspire change, and create a more equitable world. As Washington State’s first art museum, the Henry is internationally recognized for groundbreaking exhibitions, dynamic programs, and a long-standing commitment to championing artists at every stage of their careers. With a collection of nearly 30,000 objects and a deep partnership with the University of Washington, the Henry serves as a welcoming cultural hub, amplifying a diverse range of artistic voices and offering transformative, first-hand experiences that spark curiosity and new perspectives. **Admission to the Henry is free to all visitors.**. The Henry is located on the University of Washington campus, roughly at the cross streets of 15th Ave NE and NE 41st St. There is usually street parking nearby, or you can park in the UW Central Parking Garage (for a fee weekdays; Saturday afternoons and Sundays are free).
